comment.body:
Ya instead of saying “Therefore, my current tactic is a short vol trade, as I written premarket, without any directional take.”, and then buying directional long dated calls in the background, maybe say “I’m continuing to hold my last tranche of puts from 6100, and am building up a longer dated expiry of call positions to capture the highly likely bounce into March OPEX”.

I would’ve literally bought NVDA calls yesterday without being subscribed here. If she added that context yesterday that would help to bring a lot of clarity as to how she’s implementing that data.

But to say she was just “short vol trade, without any direction take” was not accurate considering she’s profitable on those calls.

Very frustrating cuz I was heavily considering buying NVDA calls for next week expiry but didn’t do so because of this commentary.

And you can look at my posts on yesterday’s thread about NVDA so see what I mean.

comment.body:
I do have sympathy for this view. I think what Alma does isidentify different paths, based on combinations of different conditions. Therefore she's not necessarily wrong to say "as expected", because when the conditions change, the path changes.
However most of the time she clearly has a preferred path, and I find it quite disingenuous for her or her followers to say everything is perfectly predicted even when the preferred path changes. Good example is when we were hovering above 6100. Alma’s lean was more up to come, but I was pointing out that repeated failure to sustain new highs looked like institutional distribution and was not bullish. I was told not to be so “one-dimensional” 😊. When the market rolled over, it was “as expected”.
The talent is clearly there. For me the key is clarity and transparency. No one is perfect and no one should be expected to be perfect.

comment.body:
plp were bearish in february, 
i kept buying all the dips, and we took the market up to new all-time highs
some respected and followed furus kept posting about supportive feb opex, and i said, nah, it will be suppressive, market went down, but it went down on wednesday prior, where i signaled you put.
then spot decay, in the last two  weeks catched every intraday bottom real time, -2 trade in small loss.
and on tuesday i pushed this bottoming narrative, and signaled logn at 5532, even posted it on twitter later... 

but bcs the price fluctuations aka risk is high, i don't really like longer-term trades too much
